title: digitalis purpurea subsp. thapsi
intro: Digitalis purpurea subsp. thapsi, also known as Foxglove, is a subspecies of the Foxglove plant that is native to the Mediterranean region. It contains the glycoside digoxin, which has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including increasing heart muscle contractions and reducing heart rate. The health benefits of Digitalis purpurea subsp. thapsi include its ability to treat conditions such as atrial fibrillation and congestive heart failure, as well as its potential to lower blood pressure and improve symptoms of heart failure. In herbal preparations, Digitalis purpurea subsp. thapsi is often used in tinctures, infusions, and capsules, but it should be used under the guidance of a healthcare professional due to its potential toxicity.
